cvs_standard: All entities source-attributed. No pattern-inferred entities.

pattern_inferred_false_positives.reason: Matched Malay verb 'to unite' in ohbulancom_20260713 line 93: 'kita semua bersatu untuk menikmati hidangan KFC'. NOT the Bersatu party. Same false positive as 20260711 and 20260712.

Malformed regex capture from bhariancom_20260713 line 27: 'BN Johor perlu buktikan kuasai 48 DUN bukan cek kosong'. 'DUN bukan' is not a constituency name — it is a fragment of '48 DUN bukan cek kosong'.

script_compliance_audit.case_variant_duplicates: 12 raw party entries are case-variant duplicates of 4 unique strings (umno/UMNO/Umno, bn/BN/Barisan Nasional, PAS/Pas/pas, pn/PN) + 1 false positive (bersatu). Deduplicated unique legitimate parties: 3 NS-relevant (PAS, Umno, BN) + 1 non-NS (PN, Johor context from kosmo 404 headline).

pn_reference_context.context: Kekalahan dijangka, Pengerusi PN digesa letak jawatan
pn_reference_context.note: Real PN party reference but from kosmo 404 page article-link headline about Johor PRN aftermath (PN chairman urged to resign after expected defeat), NOT NS PRN context. Not NS-relevant.

script_compliance_audit.constituency_regex_result: 1 capture ('DUN bukan') — false positive. Zero of NS's 36 DUN constituencies matched.
script_compliance_audit.missing_source_attribution: No entity in any script output file carries a source file reference. All entities are bare strings.
script_compliance_audit.verdict: Script output is NON-COMPLIANT with CVS standard (pattern-inferred false positives, no source attribution, 5/7 categories empty by design, case-variant duplication).

borderline_references_excluded.reason: Appears ONLY in image asset filename 'TB_Hadi Awang_SINAR.jpg' accompanying 'PRN Negeri Sembilan: Pas akui berunding dengan BN' on astroawani. NOT in astroawani article prose. However, Abdul Hadi Awang IS attributed from thestarcommy article prose (headline + dateline text), so he is included in verified politicians via thestar source. Consistent with 20260711/20260712 standard (prose-only party/person attribution).

key_finding: BREAKTHROUGH: After TWO consecutive days (20260711, 20260712) with ZERO NS PRN election content, the 20260713 collection contains SIGNIFICANT Negeri Sembilan PRN 2026 election intelligence. Three sources (thestar, astroawani, bharian) carry directly NS-relevant political reporting: (1) PAS President Abdul Hadi Awang claims PAS is in talks with Umno for NS PRN cooperation (thestar, astroawani); (2) PAS offers UMNO to lead Negeri Sembilan if they win PRN (bharian); (3) BN's Johor victory will boost morale and momentum for NS PRN, says Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias from Seremban (thestar); (4) Analysts caution Johor sweep won't guarantee domino effect in NS and GE16 (thestar). 12 NS election-relevant entities identified across politicians (2), parties (3), issues (5), events (1), and locations (1). Key political dynamics emerging: PAS-BN/UMNO electoral cooperation for NS PRN, with PAS potentially ceding NS leadership to UMNO. NS PRN scheduled for Aug 1, 2026 (~19 days away). Johor PRN results (BN decisive victory) serving as momentum narrative. The Star maintains a 'Negri Crisis' topic tag and 'State Polls 2026' tag, indicating ongoing NS political coverage. Collection quality improved: 3 of 7 sources have NS-relevant content vs 0 of 9 on 20260712 and 0 of 10 on 20260711.

sentiment_analysis_readiness.reason: 12 NS election-relevant entities found across politicians (Hadi Awang, Jalaluddin Alias), parties (PAS, UMNO, BN), issues (PAS-Umno cooperation, PAS offers UMNO NS leadership, BN Johor momentum, Johor domino effect, Negri Crisis), events (NS PRN Aug 1), and locations (Seremban). These entities are directly attributable to NS PRN 2026 political dynamics and suitable for sentiment analysis.
recommended_sentiment_targets: PAS-BN/UMNO cooperation narrative (positive/negative framing across sources)
BN Johor victory momentum transfer to NS (optimism vs caution)
PAS offering UMNO NS leadership (strategic concession vs weakness)
Analysts' domino effect caution (skepticism toward Johor results predicting NS outcome)
Abdul Hadi Awang statements (credibility and reception)
Jalaluddin Alias BN momentum claims (confidence vs overconfidence)
sentiment_analysis_readiness.recommendation: Data is READY for NS PRN 2026 sentiment analysis. Recommend running sentiment analysis on the 3 NS-relevant source files (thestar, astroawani, bharian) with focus on PAS-BN cooperation framing, BN momentum narrative, and analyst caution about domino effect. Cross-reference with 20260712 Johor PRN results sentiment for momentum transfer analysis.
